UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile in the Sunshine application, including their financial balance and other personal settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, which is always 'user'.

saldoUSDT.description: The user's current investment balance in USDT (Tether). This field is read-only for users and can only be modified through approved deposit requests.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'es' for Spanish, 'en' for English).

termsAccepted.description: Indicates whether the user has accepted the 'Términos y Condiciones de Inversión de Sunshine' during registration.

invitationCodeUsedId.description: Reference to the InvitationCode entity that was used by this user during registration. (Relationship: InvitationCode 1:N UserProfile)

ultimoCheckIn.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of the user's last daily check-in.

walletAddress.description: The user's unique USDT (TRC-20) wallet address, used to prevent multi-accounting.

planActivo.description: The amount of the user's active investment plan in USDT. 0 if no active plan.

fechaInicioPlan.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of when the user's active investment plan started. Can be null.

AdminProfile.title: AdminProfile
AdminProfile.description: Represents an administrator's profile in the Sunshine application.

uid.description: Unique Firebase Authentication UID for the administrator.

name.description: The full name of the admin.

InvitationCode.title: InvitationCode
InvitationCode.description: Represents an invitation code required for new user registration in the Sunshine application. Each code can be used only once.

id.description: The unique invitation code string itself, serving as its identifier (e.g., 'SUNSHINE2023').

isUsed.description: Indicates whether this invitation code has been used to register a user.

usedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that used this invitation code. Only present if 'isUsed' is true. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 InvitationCode)

createdAt.description: The date and time when this invitation code was created.

DepositRequest.title: DepositRequest
DepositRequest.description: Represents a user's request to deposit USDT into their Sunshine investment account, awaiting administrator review and approval.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DepositRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that made this deposit request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DepositRequest)

amountUSDT.description: The amount of USDT (Tether) the user intends to deposit.

comprobanteURL.description: The URL to the uploaded proof of transfer (comprobante) for the deposit.

status.description: The current status of the deposit request (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected').

requestDate.description: The date and time when the deposit request was submitted.

adminNotes.description: Optional internal notes added by an administrator regarding the approval or rejection of the request.

processedByAdminId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (admin) who processed this request. Only present if status is 'approved' or 'rejected'. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DepositRequest)

processedDate.description: The date and time when the deposit request was processed by an administrator.

WalletAddress.title: WalletAddress
WalletAddress.description: Represents a unique wallet address to prevent multi-accounting. The document ID is the wallet address itself.

userId.description: The UID of the user this wallet belongs to.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the wallet was registered.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID (`userId`) corresponds to a Firebase Authentication UID. 'saldoUSDT' is read-only for users.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID.

definition.description: Stores administrator profiles. This collection is used to grant admin privileges across the application.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID for the admin.

definition.description: Stores unique invitation codes. Unauthenticated users can perform a `get()` to verify a specific code during registration.

params.name: invitationCodeId
params.description: The unique invitation code string itself.

definition.description: Stores deposit requests specific to a user. The 'userId' field within each document denormalizes ownership context for security rules. Users can create and read their own requests.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID of the user submitting the request.

params.name: depositRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the deposit request.

definition.description: Stores unique wallet addresses to prevent multi-accounting. Used during registration to check for uniqueness. Document ID is the wallet address.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to core design principles. A critical aspect is achieving Authorization Independence to eliminate slow and complex `get()` calls in security rules.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **User-Owned Subcollections:** For user-specific data like `DepositRequest`, the `userId` is explicitly included as a field within each `DepositRequest` document (`/users/{userId}/deposit_requests/{depositRequestId}`). This denormalizes the ownership context, allowing rules to directly evaluate `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without needing to inspect the parent `users/{userId}` document. This ensures that creating or updating a `DepositRequest` is an atomic operation and not dependent on the parent document's attributes for authorization.

**Support for Query-Able Access Patterns (QAPs):**
1.  **User Data (UserProfile):** Individual users can access their own profile at `/users/$(request.auth.uid)`.
2.  **Invitation Codes (InvitationCode):** The `/invitation_codes/{invitationCodeId}` collection allows unauthenticated users to perform a `get()` operation on a specific code during registration to verify its existence and `isUsed` status.
3.  **Deposit Requests (DepositRequest):** Users can `list` their own deposit requests at `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/deposit_requests`.

**Structural Segregation and Consistency:**
*   Each collection adheres to a homogeneous security posture. `users` holds all user profiles, `invitation_codes` manages code lifecycle, and `deposit_requests` (within user subcollections) manages user-specific deposit activities. This simplifies security rules greatly by associating clear access patterns with specific paths.
